extra.reasoning:
Property: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b).

Let C = the 12-way conjunction (low_ejection_fraction AND renal_impairment AND ckd_dialysis AND bradycardia AND prior_gi_bleed AND heart_failure AND diabetes AND peptic_ulcer AND elderly AND hyperkalemia AND immunosuppressed AND hepatic_impairment).

rec_drug_a: Only rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50) can set it true, and only when C is true. Otherwise default makes it false. So rec_drug_a = true iff C.

rec_drug_b: rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) sets it true when low_ejection_fraction; guard_suppress_b (priority 90) sets it false when C. Default false.

Consider the only case where rec_drug_a can be true: C true. C true implies low_ejection_fraction true, so rec_b_from_trigger matches (would set b := true), but guard_suppress_b also matches with higher priority 90 > 50, so per priority conflict resolution it wins and rec_drug_b := false. Thus whenever rec_drug_a is true, rec_drug_b is forced false.

When C is false, rec_drug_a is false, so the conjunction (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is false regardless of rec_drug_b.

Therefore rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b is never simultaneously true across the entire input space. The higher-priority guard exactly covers the only scenario where a is set. Property holds.
